title_en: Is Donald Trump serious in declaring the ceasefire with Iran over?
title_zh: 特朗普宣布与伊朗停火的表态是不是认真的？
rubric_en: Iran’s ultra-nationalist new leaders may welcome his bluster
rubric_zh: 伊朗新一代强硬民族主义领导人或许会欢迎他的强硬姿态

paragraphs.en: It was less a traditional lament for a slain Shia holy man than a victory parade, a display of power and a rallying cry for another confrontation with America. Over six days, Iranians marked the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former supreme leader, by chanting “Death to America”. Red flags of vengeance fluttered above the crowds. Mourners hurled stones at images of Donald Trump and hung effigies of America’s president.
paragraphs.zh: 这更像是一场纪念已故什叶派圣人的传统悼念，也更像是一次胜利游行、力量展示，以及向美国发起下一次对峙的号召。伊朗人在长达六天的悼念中，以“美国死”口号送别伊朗前最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊，复仇的红旗在群众头顶猎猎飘扬。哀悼者向唐纳德·特朗普的画像投掷石块，并悬挂起了美国总统的替身。

paragraphs.en: Then, on the third and fourth days of mourning, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (irgc), the regime’s elite defenders, launched drone attacks on three tankers that they claimed had ignored their designated route through the Strait of Hormuz. If the generals meant to provoke Mr Trump, they seemed to succeed. On July 7th he ordered strikes on Iran and revoked the sanctions waiver covering Iran’s oil exports. “Scum,” he called Iran’s rulers at a nato summit in Ankara on July 8th. “It’s just a waste of time dealing with them.” Perhaps, the president suggested, America could do without a deal and simply hit Iran “hard” again.
paragraphs.zh: 随后在哀悼的第三和第四天，伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）——该政权的精英武装——对三艘油轮发动了无人机袭击，称它们未遵循霍尔木兹海峡的指定航线。如果这些将领意在挑衅特朗普总统，看来他们确实成功了。特朗普在7月7日下令对伊朗发动打击，并取消了覆盖伊朗石油出口的制裁豁免。7月8日，他在安卡拉一次北约峰会上称伊朗统治者是“败类”，并说“与他们打交道只是浪费时间”。或许，总统示意说，美国可以不必达成协议，而是再次对伊朗“下重拳”。

paragraphs.en: America carried out dozens of strikes in southern Iran overnight on July 7th, and again on July 8th. Even so, Mr Trump’s words may be bluster. American and Iranian negotiators are still expected to resume talks on July 11th in Islamabad, Pakistan’s capital, over a final agreement about Iran’s nuclear programme. But both sides look belligerent. The leadership that has emerged in Iran since Khamenei’s death seems keener to project strength and wear down Mr Trump through military pressure, rather than diplomacy.
paragraphs.zh: 美国在7月7日夜间对伊朗南部进行了数十次打击，7月8日又再次发动袭击。尽管如此，特朗普的言辞仍可能只是威吓。预计美伊谈判代表仍将在7月11日于巴基斯坦首都伊斯兰堡恢复会谈，讨论伊朗核计划最终协议。然而双方都显得咄咄逼人。自哈梅内伊去世后出现的伊朗领导层似乎更想通过军事压力来展示实力并消耗特朗普，而不是诉诸外交。

paragraphs.en: Iran responded to both sets of American strikes with attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait. The oil price reached $80 a barrel early on July 9th; Iran’s rial slipped. Observers again predicted Iran could stomach more economic pain than the global economy. “We don’t fold,” posted Iran’s chief negotiator and parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, on July 8th.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗对美方两轮打击作出回应，袭击了巴林和科威特。7月9日清晨，油价已升至每桶80美元；伊朗里亚尔走弱。观察人士再次预测伊朗能够承受的经济压力可能高于世界经济体系本身所能承受的压力。伊朗首席谈判代表兼议会议长穆罕默德·巴赫拉夫在7月8日发帖写道：“我们不会屈服。”

paragraphs.en: The funeral offered an early glimpse of the new configuration ruling Iran and what some Iranians now call a second republic. Gone were the traditional rites that marked the burial of Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, the Islamic Republic’s founder, in 1989. His body was hurried to its grave, three days after his death, wrapped in a simple white shroud. By contrast, officials spent 126 days preparing for Khamenei’s final rites, which they claimed was the largest state funeral in history. His coffin travelled for six days over 2,600km, across five cities and two countries, including Iraq.
paragraphs.zh: 这场葬礼让人们率先看到伊朗新的权力格局，也就是许多伊朗人如今称之为“第二共和国”。在其中，1989年伊朗伊斯兰共和国创始人霍梅尼大阿亚图拉（阿亚图拉·鲁霍拉·霍梅尼）葬礼上的传统仪式已不复存在。他的遗体在去世三天后匆匆入葬，包裹着朴素的白色裹尸布。相比之下，官员们为哈梅内伊的国葬准备了126天，称其为历史上规模最大的国葬。其灵柩在2600公里、五座城市和两国（包括伊拉克）之间的路程中被运送了六天。

paragraphs.en: The ceremony also revealed a shift in the regime’s centre of gravity. The irgc generals who directed the war occupied pride of place. Iranian flags dominated the stage and draped the coffins. Khamenei’s black turban—a symbol of the clerical establishment he led for 37 years—rested almost inconspicuously on top. More than 100 days after assuming power, his son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei, has still not appeared in public. Even if he eventually does, he may prove little more than a figurehead. His televised statements sound like military communiqués, not sermons. Along the funeral route, guards units and allied Iraqi militias marshalled the crowds and distributed food and water. Iran’s rulers appear to be burying not only their supreme leader but also the clerical order over which he presided.
paragraphs.zh: 这场仪式也显示出该政权重心的转移。指挥了战争的伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）将军占据了核心位置。伊朗国旗主导了整个舞台，并覆盖在灵柩上。哈梅内伊那顶黑色的僧袍帽——象征他执掌三十七年的神职体系——几乎不显眼地置于顶部。上台仅百余天后，他的儿子和继承人哈梅内伊·伊斯兰教长莫加达巴（Mojtaba Khamenei）仍未公开露面。即便他最终出现，也可能只是个象征性人物。他在电视上的讲话更像是军事公报，而不是一篇教士式的布道。沿着葬礼路线，警卫部队和盟友伊拉克民兵组织调度了人群，并分发食物和饮用水。伊朗的统治者似乎不仅在埋葬他们的最高领袖，也在埋葬他所统治下的神权秩序。

paragraphs.en: Khamenei’s killing has accelerated Iran’s transition from a theocracy to an ambitious nationalistic state dominated by military men. The irgc appears to wield power with few constraints. Clerics who challenged its influence—including former Presidents Mohammad Khatami and Hassan Rouhani—were conspicuously absent from the processions.
paragraphs.zh: 哈梅内伊的死加速了伊朗从神权政治向由军人主导、野心勃勃的民族国家转型。伊斯兰革命卫队似乎在几乎没有制约的情况下行使权力。包括前总统穆罕默德·哈塔米（Mohammad Khatami）和哈桑·鲁哈尼（Hassan Rouhani）在内、公开挑战其影响力的神职人物，在游行队列中都显得格外缺席。

paragraphs.en: The generals, meanwhile, are asserting their regional power. As Iraq’s prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, prepared to curb pro-Iranian militias and for his first official trip to America, they directed Khamenei’s funeral procession through Iraq’s holy cities and lined its roads with armed groups. Free transport, food and lodgings, together with respect for Iran’s success in repelling a foreign attack, drew vast crowds. Many Iraqis who once resented Iranian meddling now curse America and Israel.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，这些将军正在巩固并展现其区域力量。就在伊拉克总理阿里·阿尔-扎伊迪（Ali al-Zaidi）准备取缔亲伊朗民兵，并筹备其首次正式访美之际，他们将哈梅内伊的葬礼队伍引向伊拉克的圣城，并用武装团体把道路两侧排得满满当当。免费交通、食物与住宿，再加上伊朗成功抵御外部袭击的威望，吸引了成千上万的人群。许多过去对伊朗干预心存怨恨的伊拉克人如今转而咒骂美国和以色列。

paragraphs.en: Iran’s bullying of its neighbours in the Gulf has also resumed. Delegations from Saudi Arabia and other Arab states travelled to Tehran to offer condolences despite enduring thousands of Iranian strikes during the war. Their gestures went unrewarded: more Iranian strikes have since followed. Gulf officials shrink at the determination of Tehran’s new leadership to control the Strait of Hormuz. One of them mused that it smacked of a return of imperial Persian ambition.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗对海湾周边邻国的欺凌也再次上演。尽管在战争中承受了数千次伊朗袭击，沙特阿拉伯和其他阿拉伯国家的代表团仍然前往德黑兰致以慰问。然而这种姿态并未得到回应：随后又发生了更多伊朗空袭。海湾官员对德黑兰新一届领导层决心控制霍尔木兹海峡的态度感到畏惧。有官员私下感叹，这更像是帝国式波斯野心的回潮。

paragraphs.en: Two forces could yet restrain Iran’s generals. The first is military reality. America’s latest strikes underline the possibility that the war could resume in earnest. Iran’s Gulf neighbours, meanwhile, appear more willing to support a tougher American approach. They are pressing partners in Europe and China to increase pressure on Tehran while quietly highlighting both their previous covert operations against Iran and their willingness to act again.
paragraphs.zh: 还有两股力量可能会约束伊朗的将领。第一是军事现实。美国最近的打击重申了这场战争可能重新全面爆发的可能。与此同时，伊朗海湾邻国似乎更愿意支持美国更强硬的立场。他们正向欧洲和中国的伙伴施压，要求其加大对德黑兰的制约力度，同时也在暗中强调自己此前对伊朗发动过秘密行动，并表明愿意再次行动。

paragraphs.en: The second is economic. Once the funeral crowds disperse, Iran will still face a fragile and isolated economy. Sanctions-busting smuggling networks can sustain the regime, but not the wider population. Perhaps 40% of Iranians live in poverty, probably more. Much of the middle class has disappeared, while the war has pulverised the country’s industrial base and left millions jobless. Most Iranians yearn for economic recovery and relief from sanctions rather than the reimposition of America’s naval blockade and more war. National renewal will require some accommodation with Iran’s enemies, not merely more bombs and bombast. ■
paragraphs.zh: 第二股力量是经济因素。葬礼人群散去后，伊朗仍将面对一个脆弱且孤立的经济。突破制裁的走私网络可以维持政权运转，却无法支撑更广泛的民众。约40%的伊朗人生活在贫困之中，实际比例可能更高。大片中产阶级已经消失，战争也已把国家的工业基础粉碎，留下数百万人失业。大多数伊朗人更渴望经济复苏和制裁缓解，而不是美国再次实施海上封锁以及更多战争。国家振兴需要与伊朗的敌对方进行某种程度的协调，而不仅仅是更多的轰炸与威吓。■
